title: U.S. Grant House, First Floor Plan (HABS IL-1221, sheet 4)
era_or_year: house built 1859-1860 (architect William Dennison); HABS drawings documented after 1933
creator: Historic American Buildings Survey; design architect William Dennison; builders Daniel Farr and Joseph Maefefete
research_notes: First floor plan is sheet 4 (00004v.jpg) of HABS IL-1221 'Drawings'. Sheet 2 of the same item is a SITE PLAN (with plant-materials legend and adjacent visitor's center) and sheet 3 is a basement/joist plan, if those variants are wanted.
research_features: Classic Italianate plan: Parlor, Library, Dining Room, Kitchen, Pantry, Bathroom & Laundry, and a grand staircase, with full dimensions. Home of Ulysses S. Grant in Galena, Illinois.
feature_highlights: well-mannered Italianate town-house plan with a main block and rear service wing
labeled rooms: Parlor, Dining Room, Library, Kitchen, Pantry, Bathroom & Laundry
U-shaped dog-leg stair with winder treads and a scrolled curved bottom step
hatched masonry walls with many perimeter window and door openings
full running dimension chains on top, bottom, and left
north arrow plus separate feet and meter scale bars
supported_examples: straight masonry walls with thickness and junctions
rectangular rooms with room labels and clear floor area
multiple door and window openings drawn as plan symbols
linear running dimensions in imperial units
dual imperial and metric scale bars for underlay calibration
roadmap_examples: floor management (this is one of several stacked levels)
wall construction profiles/poché for masonry walls
straight-run portions of the stair as a 2D stair symbol
furniture/fixture placement for bath, laundry, and kitchen fixtures

gap_features.why: the main stair turns on winder treads with a curved scrolled bottom step, which is winding-stair geometry beyond a straight run
the sheet carries a north arrow and separate feet/meter graphic scale bars as plan annotations
